title: Constraint-laden two-day drive itinerary (judgment task)
source: Tango jobs manual test checklist: 'Location-based itinerary planning' (needs bake-off, judgment); judgment-model bake-off follow-up (DEV-57)
system: You are Sierra, a careful travel-planning assistant. Use tools when a factual route, drive-time, or detour claim depends on real map data. Do not answer route questions from mental geography when routing tools are available. Label tool-backed facts separately from synthesis.
prompt: I'm planning a two-day drive from Yachats, Oregon to Boise, Idaho in my diesel truck. Constraints: the tank gives me roughly 600 miles of range and I strongly prefer not to cut it closer than 100 miles between diesel stops; I'd rather take flatter routes over steep mountain passes when the time cost is reasonable; I want to leave around 9am, stop overnight somewhere with food and lodging around 6-7pm, and arrive in Boise by mid-afternoon the next day. Compare at least two route options, recommend one with a fuel-stop strategy and an overnight stop, and be explicit about which parts are verified versus your judgment.
successCriteria: Calls osrm_route and grounds distance/duration claims in its output.
Compares at least two distinct route options before recommending one.
Fuel-stop strategy respects the 600-mile range with the stated 100-mile buffer (arithmetic consistent with tool-reported distances).
Overnight stop falls plausibly within the stated 9am-to-6/7pm first-day window given tool-reported drive times.
Clearly separates tool-verified facts from judgment/synthesis (terrain, lodging, preferences).
knownFailureModes: Answers from mental geography without osrm_route.
Recommends a single route without comparing alternatives.
Fuel math ignores the range buffer or invents exact station locations as verified facts.
Overnight stop inconsistent with tool-reported drive times from the stated departure.
Presents elevation/terrain claims as verified when no tool supports them.

rubric.name: tool_grounding
rubric.description: Route, distance, and duration claims are backed by osrm_route output; no fabricated verification.

rubric.name: constraint_reasoning
rubric.description: Fuel-range buffer, departure/overnight timing, and terrain preference are all explicitly reasoned about with consistent arithmetic.

rubric.name: plan_quality
rubric.description: The recommended itinerary is practical and actionable: clear route choice with tradeoffs, fuel-stop strategy, overnight corridor, day-two arrival.

rubric.name: honesty
rubric.description: Verified facts, estimates, and judgment are clearly distinguished; uncertainty acknowledged where tools were not used.
